message:
perf: pre-convert expert x to f32 once, drop per-load fp16 widen

The fp16-input int4 matmul widens each x load from fp16 to f32
inside the inner MAC loop. For the MoE gate_up hot path every x
column is read ~N times per call, so the widening work compounds.

New kernel `convert_fp16_to_f32` bulk-widens a contiguous buffer;
expert dispatch gathers the expert's tokens into fp16, widens once
into an f32 scratch, then calls the existing f32-input int4 matmul.

Node forward (T=61): 779 → 459 ms (-41%).
Browser bench:
  short  141 → 92 ms   (0.21× → 0.32× vs tjs)
  medium 390 → 237 ms  (0.10× → 0.16×)
  long   902 → 530 ms  (0.06× → 0.11×)

Parity unchanged: 8/8 argmax, max logit diff 0.97.
